era_context: Egypt's Old Kingdom, Dynasty IV, reign of Khafre. The Giza pyramid complex was in active use. Khafre's pyramid and mortuary complex were under construction or recently completed. This was the apex of Old Kingdom centralised power. In Mesopotamia, Sumerian city-states dominated; the Akkad Empire was not yet hegemonic. The Indus Valley Civilisation was in its early urban phase.

events.title: Khafre's reign; continuation of Giza pyramid complex use and maintenance
events.description: During the mid-reign of Khafre (c. 2558–2532 BCE), the Giza pyramid complex remained the focal point of pharaonic power and religious ideology. Khafre's pyramid and mortuary complex were in construction or recently completed. The pharaonic state continued to command vast resources and labour for monumental building projects. This year represents the height of Old Kingdom centralised power and architectural achievement.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Giza pyramid complex

events.certainty_note: Khafre's regnal dates are traditional estimates with ±10–20 year uncertainty. The year 2556 BCE falls within his reign by conventional chronology.
cross_references: Part of Dynasty IV pyramid-building programme

disconfirming_evidence: The chronological dating of Khafre's reign is uncertain by ±10–20 years. Some alternative chronologies would place 2556 BCE in a different reign. The Sothic cycle calculations that underpin Old Kingdom chronology are debated among Egyptologists.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 2556 BCE is sparse and indirect, relying on king lists and archaeological evidence. No contemporary written narratives exist for this specific year. Dating is based on regnal lengths and astronomical calculations with inherent uncertainties. The surviving sources are biased toward royal monuments and elite perspectives.
